title: 2026 FIFA World Cup Winner
description:
This market will resolve according to the national team that wins the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

If at any point it becomes impossible for this team to win the FIFA World Cup based on the rules of FIFA (e.g., they are eliminated in the knockout stage), this market will resolve immediately to “No”.

If the 2026 FIFA World Cup is permanently canceled or has not been completed by October 13, 2026, 11:59 PM this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source will be official information from FIFA, however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Spain leads Polymarket's 2026 FIFA World Cup winner consensus at 17.4% implied probability, propelled by their Euro 2024 triumph and dominant UEFA qualifiers, narrowly ahead of France (16.1%) whose squad depth and Nations League form sustain contention. England (11.3%) trails closely with Premier League-fueled talent and steady qualification, while defending champions Argentina (8.9%) and perennial powerhouse Brazil (8.6%) round out the top five amid South American resilience. The bunched probabilities highlight competitive dynamics in the expanded 48-team field—finalized via April 1 playoffs—including host advantages for USA, Canada, and Mexico, Norway's Haaland threat, and historical knockout upsets, leaving traders wary of any single favorite amid balanced continental strength and two months until group stage openers.

title: Next Prime Minister of Hungary
description:
Parliamentary elections are expected to be held in Hungary on April 12, 2026.

This market will resolve to the individual who is next officially appointed and confirmed as Prime Minister of Hungary following the 2026 parliamentary election.

To count for resolution, the individual must be formally elected and appointed to the role of Prime Minister. Any interim or caretaker Prime Minister will not count toward the resolution of this market.

If no such Prime Minister is confirmed by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “Other.”

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the Government of Hungary; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Péter Magyar's Tisza party secured a landslide victory in Hungary's April 12, 2026, parliamentary election, with record 77.8% voter turnout driving the outcome and prompting incumbent Viktor Orbán's immediate concession, propelling trader consensus to 98.9% on Magyar as next prime minister. This seismic shift ends Orbán's 16-year rule amid widespread public demand for change, as reflected in pre-election polls showing Tisza's commanding lead. Formal government formation awaits National Assembly confirmation, where Tisza's majority seats position Magyar to claim the premiership. Rare challenges could arise from result disputes, recounts, or coalition hurdles, though none have materialized amid international acclaim for the democratic milestone.

title: Nuggets vs. Spurs
description:
In the upcoming NBA game, scheduled for April 12 at 8:30PM ET:
If the Nuggets win, the market will resolve to "Nuggets".
If the Spurs win, the market will resolve to "Spurs".
If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed.
If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50-50.
The result will be determined based on the final score including any overtime periods.

eventMetadata.context_description: As the NBA regular season finale approaches, the Denver Nuggets enter their matchup against the San Antonio Spurs hampered by an extensive injury report, with Nikola Jokic listed as questionable for injury management, Jamal Murray, Aaron Gordon, Christian Braun, Peyton Watson, Tim Hardaway Jr., and others ruled out. This rest approach protects playoff health amid a locked-in third seed in the Western Conference standings (53-28 record), contrasting the Spurs' elite positioning at 62-19 and second place. Following Denver's thrilling 136-134 overtime victory over San Antonio on April 4—powered by Jokic's 40 points—Spurs leverage home-court advantage, Victor Wembanyama's interior dominance, and fresher depth to capitalize on Nuggets' absences in a low-stakes finale.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket assigns California Governor Gavin Newsom a 27.3% implied probability as the 2028 Democratic presidential nominee, far ahead in a fragmented field, driven by a recent Punchbowl News survey where 75% of Washington insiders deemed him best positioned to lead the party against President Trump's second term. His edge stems from massive fundraising networks, national media clashes with Trump—including recent personal attacks over dyslexia—and a shadow campaign building executive credentials. Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez trails at 8.5% on progressive grassroots energy and generational appeal, while Jon Ossoff and Kamala Harris tie at 6.2%, with Ossoff rising on Georgia battleground profile despite Harris leading some March polls. Consolidation could follow strong 2026 midterm performances, key endorsements, or fundraising hauls ahead of Iowa caucuses and early primaries.

title: Peru Presidential Election Winner
description:
General elections are scheduled to be held in Peru on April 12, 2026.

This market will resolve according to the listed candidate who wins the next Peruvian Presidential election. 

This market includes any potential second round. 

If the results are not known definitively by October 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "Other".

This market will resolve based on the results of this election, as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve solely on the official results as reported by the Peruvian government, specifically the National Office of Electoral Processes (Oficina Nacional de Procesos Electorales, ONPE) (https://www.onpe.gob.pe/elecciones/) and the National Jury of Elections (Jurado Nacional de Elecciones, JNE) (https://portal.jne.gob.pe/portal/)

If the results are not known definitively by October 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "Other".

This market will resolve based on the results of this election, as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve solely on the official results as reported by the Peruvian government, specifically the National Office of Electoral Processes (Oficina Nacional de Procesos Electorales, ONPE) (https://www.onpe.gob.pe/elecciones/) and the National Jury of Elections (Jurado Nacional de Elecciones, JNE) (https://portal.jne.gob.pe/portal/)

eventMetadata.context_description: Peru's first-round presidential election on April 12 featured a fragmented field of 35 candidates, with no one poised for a majority and a potential June 7 runoff looming. Early official counts covering under 25% of precincts show Rafael López Aliaga leading at around 23%, followed closely by Keiko Fujimori and surging independent Jorge Nieto, amid voting extensions in populous Lima delaying full tallies. Trader consensus favors Fujimori's 43.5% implied probability to claim the presidency, buoyed by exit polls placing her ahead and her consistent pre-election polling strength among right-leaning voters disillusioned by a decade of instability, while López Aliaga's 30% reflects rural gains and Nieto’s 15% a late centrist surge. Urban results and turnout in battlegrounds could tip top-two positioning.

title: Elon Musk # tweets April 7 - April 14, 2026?
description:
This market will resolve according to the number of times Elon Musk (@elonmusk), posts on X from April 7 12:00 PM ET to April 14, 2026 12:00 PM ET.

For the purposes of this market, only main feed posts, quote posts and reposts will count.

Replies will NOT count towards the total - however, replies on the main feed such as https://x.com/elonmusk/status/1786073478711353576 will be counted by the tracker.

Deleted posts will count as long as they remain available long enough to be captured by the tracker (~5 minutes).

Community reposts which are not counted by the tracker not count toward the total.

The resolution source for this market is the 'Post Counter' figure for posts found at https://xtracker.polymarket.com. Individual posts can be viewed by clicking "Export Data". If the tracker does not update correctly in accordance with the rules, X itself may be used as a secondary resolution source.

title: Hawks vs. Heat
description:
In the upcoming NBA game, scheduled for April 12 at 6:00PM ET:
If the Hawks win, the market will resolve to "Hawks".
If the Heat win, the market will resolve to "Heat".

eventMetadata.context_description: In the NBA regular-season finale on April 12, trader consensus heavily favored the Miami Heat after Atlanta rested key contributors like Jalen Johnson and CJ McCollum despite needing a win to clinch the East's No. 5 seed and avoid the play-in tournament. Hawks' injury report listed seven players out, including Onyeka Okongwu (finger) and Dyson Daniels (toe), thinning their rotation following a momentum-building win over Cleveland. Miami, with play-in positioning locked, missed Nikola Jovic (ankle) and Simone Fontecchio but had Tyler Herro and Norman Powell available, leveraging home-court advantage and a prior 128-97 rout of Atlanta in February to secure a blowout victory and cap their campaign.

eventMetadata.context_description: With both the Golden State Warriors and Los Angeles Clippers locked into Western Conference play-in tournament spots as the regular-season finale on April 12, trader consensus reflects the low-stakes nature of the matchup at Intuit Dome, where coaches are prioritizing rest ahead of postseason prep. Warriors star Stephen Curry is questionable with a recent ankle injury after exiting the prior game early, Draymond Green is questionable (back), and new small-ball lineup experiments signal rotation tweaks. Clippers' Kawhi Leonard is confirmed out, thinning their frontcourt, while no major new injuries emerged in final reports. Minimal motivation tempers expectations, with focus shifting to play-in seeding implications and health preservation for elimination games.

title: What will WTI Crude Oil (WTI) hit in April 2026?
description: What will WTI Crude Oil (WTI) hit in April 2026?

markets.question: Will WTI Crude Oil (WTI) hit (HIGH) $160 in April?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if, at any point during April 2026, any 1-minute candle for the Active Month of WTI Crude Oil futures has a final "High" price equal to or above the listed price. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

For WTI futures, the active month refers to the nearest listed contract month. The active month changes at 6:00:00 PM ET at the start of the trading session two business days prior to that contract's last trading day, at which point the next listed contract becomes the active month.

For WTI Crude Oil (CL) futures, the last trading day is defined as three business days prior to the 25th calendar day of the month preceding the contract's delivery month (or four business days prior if the 25th calendar day is not a business day), consistent with CME contract specifications.

Only prices achieved during the applicable trading session for the underlying market will be considered. Under the standard schedule, trading is open from 6:00:00 PM ET Sunday through 5:00:00 PM ET Friday, with a daily break from 5:00:00 PM ET to 6:00:00 PM ET, except where modified by holiday or special-session hours as listed on Pyth.

Prices will be used exactly as published by Pyth, without rounding.

If the Active Month contract does not trade at all during the listed time frame, this market will resolve to "No".

In the event of a contract specification change, feed change, or similar structural modification affecting the underlying market during the listed time frame, this market will resolve based on adjusted prices as displayed on Pyth.

The resolution source for this market is Pyth — specifically, the Active Month WTI Crude Oil futures "High" prices available at https://pythdata.app/explore?search=WTI, with the chart settings configured for 1-minute candles.

Historical 1-minute candles may be accessed by appending a Unix timestamp (seconds) to the Pyth chart URL using the "t=" parameter.

If the relevant Pyth data is unavailable due to a system outage, data failure, or other technical disruption that prevents verification of the required 1-minute candle data, the official daily high price published for the Active Month WTI Crude Oil (CL) futures contract by CME Group may be used to determine whether the listed price was reached during the applicable trading session.

eventMetadata.context_description: WTI crude oil spot prices have surged above $105 per barrel as of April 13, propelled by escalating U.S.-Iran tensions, including threats of port blockades in the Strait of Hormuz and collapsed peace talks, overriding bearish fundamentals. The EIA's April 8 report showed U.S. crude inventories rising 3.1 million barrels to 464.7 million for the week ending April 3—exceeding expectations—amid slightly lower refinery inputs at 16.3 million barrels per day. OPEC+ initiated a modest 206,000 barrels per day output increase this month from eight key producers, yet supply risks dominate trader consensus. Watch the April 15 EIA data for the week ending April 10 and any Middle East developments, which could amplify volatility through month-end.

title: Fed decision in April?
description:
The FED interest rates are defined in this market by the upper bound of the target federal funds range. The decisions on the target federal fund range are made by the Federal Open Market Committee (FOMC) meetings.

This market will resolve to the amount of basis points the upper bound of the target federal funds rate is changed by versus the level it was prior to the Federal Reserve's April 2026 meeting.

If the target federal funds rate is changed to a level not expressed in the displayed options, the change will be rounded up to the nearest 25 and will resolve to the relevant bracket. (e.g. if there's a cut/increase of 12.5 bps it will be considered to be 25 bps)

The resolution source for this market is the FOMC’s statement after its meeting scheduled for April 28-29, 2026 according to the official calendar: https://www.federalreserve.gov/monetarypolicy/fomccalendars.htm.

The level and change of the target federal funds rate is also published at the official website of the Federal Reserve at https://www.federalreserve.gov/monetarypolicy/openmarket.htm.

This market may resolve as soon as the FOMC’s statement for their April meeting with relevant data is issued. If no statement is released by the end date of the next scheduled meeting, this market will resolve to the "No change" bracket.

eventMetadata.context_description: Polymarket traders, wagering real capital, price a 98.2% implied probability of no Federal Reserve rate change at the April 28-29 FOMC meeting, reflecting consensus on a steady Fed funds target amid surging inflation pressures. March 2026 CPI jumped 0.9% month-over-month to 3.3% year-over-year—driven by record gas price spikes tied to geopolitical tensions—while nonfarm payrolls added 178,000 jobs, dropping unemployment to 4.3% and underscoring labor market resilience. This data has dashed rate-cut hopes (now <1.5%) and hike prospects, aligning with CME FedWatch futures showing steady rates around 3.6%. A dramatic economic downturn or oil stabilization could challenge this positioning ahead of the decision.

title: 2026 NBA Champion
description: This market is to predict the winner of the 2025–26 NBA Finals.

markets.question: Will the Oklahoma City Thunder win the 2026 NBA Finals?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the Oklahoma City Thunder win the 2026 NBA Finals. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

This market will resolve to “No” if it becomes impossible for this team to win the 2026 NBA Finals based off the rules of the NBA.

The resolution source for this market will be information from the NBA.

eventMetadata.context_description: Oklahoma City Thunder lead trader consensus at 41.5% implied probability for the 2026 NBA championship after clinching the Western Conference's No. 1 seed with a league-best 64-17 record, fueled by Shai Gilgeous-Alexander's MVP-caliber campaign topping win shares and a deep young core delivering elite defense and transition scoring amid minimal injuries. San Antonio Spurs sit second at 16.2% following their 62-19 surge behind Victor Wembanyama's dominance, positioning them for a potential first-round clash with OKC while boasting home-court edge through the conference semifinals. Boston Celtics (11.3%) and Denver Nuggets (10.5%) round out top challengers with strong playoff pedigrees—defending champs and two-time finalists, respectively—but trail due to East's relative parity and recent seeding locks like Detroit Pistons atop the conference at 59-22 despite postseason inexperience. Playoffs tip off April 18 after play-in games, with OKC's rest advantage and path through West contenders driving sentiment.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket prices Robert F. Kennedy Jr. at 49% implied probability to secure the Republican presidential nomination in 2028, narrowly ahead of Vice President J.D. Vance at 40.3% and Secretary of State Marco Rubio at 21.9%, underscoring a fluid post-Trump succession race among administration insiders. RFK Jr.'s surge stems from his high-profile tenure as Health and Human Services Secretary, where a Politico poll released April 11 highlighted strong public backing for his "Make America Healthy Again" regulatory crusade against food and pharmaceutical industries, energizing populist voters. Vance holds steady as the MAGA heir apparent per a late-March CPAC straw poll win, while Rubio gains from hawkish stances amid the ongoing Iran conflict and reports of President Trump's private polling of donors favoring him over Vance. The tight contest reflects divided party factions—populist outsiders vs. establishment figures—with no primaries until 2027; midterm results, foreign policy outcomes, or Trump endorsements could create separation.

title: What price will Bitcoin hit in April?
description: What price will Bitcoin hit in April?

markets.question: Will Bitcoin dip to $60,000 in April?
markets.description:
This market will immediately resolve to "Yes" if any Binance 1 minute candle for BTC/USDT during the month specified in the title (from 00:00 AM ET on the first day to 11:59 PM ET on the last), has a final Low price equal to or lower than the price specified in the title. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No."

The resolution source for this market is Binance, specifically the BTC/USDT Low prices available at https://www.binance.com/en/trade/BTC_USDT, with the chart settings on "1m" for one-minute candles selected on the top bar.

Please note that the outcome of this market depends solely on the price data from the Binance BTC/USDT trading pair. Prices from other exchanges, different trading pairs, or spot markets will not be considered for the resolution of this market.

This market will immediately resolve to "Yes" if any Binance 1 minute candle for BTC/USDT during the month specified in the title (from 00:00 AM ET on the first day to 11:59 PM ET on the last), has a final Low price equal to or lower than the price specified in the title. eventMetadata.context_description: Vice President JD Vance holds a slim lead over California Governor Gavin Newsom in Polymarket's trader consensus for the 2028 presidential winner, reflecting a fragmented early field where no candidate exceeds 19% amid post-Trump term limits and uncertainty before the November 2026 midterms. Recent UMass Lowell polling (March 26-30) shows Vance ahead 33-30% in a head-to-head matchup, bolstered by his VP role and CPAC straw poll dominance (53%), while Newsom gains from Democratic primary surges and hypothetical general election edges in some surveys. Marco Rubio's rise to third reflects his Secretary of State visibility and market shifts closing the GOP gap. The tight race persists due to undeclared candidacies, Trump administration performance, and divided fields; separation could come from midterm control of Congress, official announcements, or Trump endorsements clarifying party paths to victory in swing states.

title: Iran x Israel/US conflict ends by...?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if there is a continuous 14-day period without any qualifying military action between Iran, and Israel and the United States that begins at any time between market creation and the specified end date (ET). Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No". 

The 14-day period may begin at any time between the creation of this market and the specified end date (ET), and must continue uninterrupted through 12:00 PM ET on the 14th calendar day after the strike is confirmed. 

A "military action" is defined as any use of force by Iran, or Israel and the United States against the other’s soil, or official embassies or consulates, that is either officially acknowledged by the acting government or confirmed through a clear consensus of credible reporting. 

This includes, but is not limited to, airstrikes, naval attacks, and ground incursions. 

Cyberattacks, sanctions, and diplomatic actions do not count. 

Only actions by Iranian forces explicitly claimed by the Islamic Republic of Iran, or confirmed to have originated from Iranian territory will qualify as Iranian military actions. Attacks on Israel or the US by proxy forces (i.e. Hezbollah, Houthis, etc.) will not count.

eventMetadata.context_description: US-Iran peace talks in Pakistan collapsed on April 12 after 21 hours without a permanent deal, despite a fragile two-week ceasefire that began around April 8 halting prior US-Israeli airstrikes on Iranian targets. US Vice President JD Vance described it as a final offer, while Tehran rejected terms demanding nuclear curbs and Strait of Hormuz reopening. The US military announced a blockade of Iranian ports starting April 13, prompting IRGC warnings of retaliation, as Hezbollah fired projectiles into Israel. Israel heightened readiness for renewed operations amid waning domestic US war support per polls. These setbacks amid stalled diplomacy signal persistent escalation risks, with no near-term resolution in sight despite trader consensus favoring prolonged tensions.

title: Military action against Iran ends by...?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if neither the US nor Israel initiates a drone, missile, or air strike on Iranian soil or any official Iranian embassy or consulate on a full calendar day by the listed date, Iran Standard Time (GMT+3:30). Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

If the date/time of a qualifying strike cannot be confirmed by a consensus of credible reporting by the end of the third calendar day after the listed date, the respective market will resolve to "Yes" regardless of whether a strike is later confirmed to have occurred. 

For the purposes of this market, a qualifying "strike" is defined as the use of aerial bombs, drones or missiles (including cruise or ballistic missiles) launched by US or Israeli military forces that impact Iranian ground territory or any official Iranian embassy or consulate (e.g., if a weapons depot on Iranian soil is hit by an US/Israeli missile, this will qualify).

Missiles or drones that are intercepted and surface-to-air missile strikes will not be considered, regardless of whether they land on Iranian territory or cause damage.

Actions such as artillery fire, small arms fire, FPV or ATGM strikes directly, ground incursions, naval shelling, cyberattacks, or other operations conducted by US/Israeli ground operatives will not be considered.

The resolution source will be a consensus of credible reporting.

Missiles or drones that are intercepted and surface-to-air missile strikes will not be considered, regardless of whether they land on Iranian territory or cause damage.

Actions such as artillery fire, small arms fire, FPV or ATGM strikes directly, ground incursions, naval shelling, cyberattacks, or other operations conducted by US/Israeli ground operatives will not be considered.

The resolution source will be a consensus of credible reporting.

series.title: Military action against Iran ends by

eventMetadata.context_description: A US naval blockade of Iranian ports and Strait of Hormuz traffic, announced by Central Command on April 12 and effective April 13 at 10am ET, tests a fragile two-week ceasefire agreed April 8—mediated by Pakistan and due to expire April 22—following collapsed Islamabad talks. President Trump described the truce as "holding well," yet limited airstrikes persist amid Israel's ongoing operations against Hezbollah in Lebanon. The conflict traces to US-Israeli strikes on February 28 targeting Iranian nuclear sites and proxies, prompting Iranian missile barrages. Traders eye Iranian retaliation to the blockade, potential escalation via proxy forces or energy infrastructure hits, and diplomatic off-ramps before the ceasefire lapses.

title: Jazz vs. Lakers
description:
In the upcoming NBA game, scheduled for April 12 at 8:30PM ET:
If the Jazz win, the market will resolve to "Jazz".
If the Lakers win, the market will resolve to "Lakers".
If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed.
If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50-50.
The result will be determined based on the final score including any overtime periods.

eventMetadata.context_description: In the NBA regular-season finale at Crypto.com Arena, the Los Angeles Lakers (52-29, vying for Western Conference seeding) host a depleted Utah Jazz squad amid critical injury developments shaping trader consensus. Lakers rule out Luka Dončić (hamstring) and Austin Reaves (oblique), with LeBron James (foot injury management) and Jaxson Hayes (foot) questionable per the latest report, potentially forcing reliance on depth against a Jazz team missing Isaiah Collier (hamstring), Kyle Filipowski (back), Keyonte George (hamstring), and others. Lakers hold strong home-court advantage and recent momentum, while Utah's tanking form and roster gaps highlight vulnerabilities; monitor pre-game updates on James for final lineup impacts in this low-stakes clash.

title: F1 Drivers' Champion
description:
This market will resolve according to the listed driver that finishes 1st in the driver standings for the 2026 F1 season.

This market will resolve as soon as the official results of the final scheduled race of the 2026 F1 season are known.

If multiple drivers tie for first place in the drivers standings, this market will resolve according to the tiebreak procedure used by F1 to determine the 2026 F1 Drivers’ champion.

If at any point it becomes impossible for a listed driver to win the 2026 F1 Drivers Championship based on the rules of F1 (e.g., they are mathematically eliminated from contention), the corresponding market will resolve to “No”.

If the F1 season is permanently canceled or has not been completed by February 28, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from Formula 1.

This market will resolve according to the listed driver that finishes 1st in the driver standings for the 2026 F1 season.

This market will resolve as soon as the official results of the final scheduled race of the 2026 F1 season are known.

If multiple drivers tie for first place in the drivers standings, this market will resolve according to the tiebreak procedure used by F1 to determine the 2026 F1 Drivers’ champion.

If at any point it becomes impossible for a listed driver to win the 2026 F1 Drivers Championship based on the rules of F1 (e.g., they are mathematically eliminated from contention), the corresponding market will resolve to “No”.

If the F1 season is permanently canceled or has not been completed by March 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from Formula 1.

eventMetadata.context_description: Mercedes' early-season dominance in the 2026 F1 constructors' standings with 135 points has positioned George Russell (44.5%) and teammate Kimi Antonelli (30.1%) as overwhelming trader consensus favorites for the drivers' championship, reflecting their combined podium sweeps in Australia, China, and Japan. Antonelli's back-to-back victories in China and Japan—coupled with pole in Suzuka—propelled the 21-year-old rookie to the top of the driver standings with 72 points, nine ahead of Russell's 63, marking him as the youngest leader ever. However, Russell's experience, qualifying prowess, and race management edge sustain his slight lead in implied probabilities amid the intensifying intra-team battle, while Ferrari's Charles Leclerc (6.3%) trails due to Mercedes' superior pace and energy deployment.

title: Eurovision Winner 2026
description:
This market will resolve to the country whose candidate for Eurovision 2026 wins.

If at any point it is impossible for the listed candidate to win Eurovision 2026 based on the rules of the competition (i.e. they are eliminated), this market may immediately resolve to "No".

If no winner is announced by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve "Other". All ties will be broken according to EBU's official Eurovision rules.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from Eurovision (https://eurovision.tv/), including live footage of Eurovision 2026, however a consensus of credible reporting will suffice.

This market will resolve to the country whose candidate for Eurovision 2026 wins.

If at any point it is impossible for the listed candidate to win Eurovision 2026 based on the rules of the competition (i.e. they are eliminated), this market may immediately resolve to "No".

If no winner is announced by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve "Other". All ties will be broken according to EBU's official Eurovision rules.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from Eurovision (https://eurovision.tv/), including live footage of Eurovision 2026, however a consensus of credible reporting will suffice.

eventMetadata.context_description: Finland's Linda Lampenius and Pete Parkkonen lead Polymarket trader consensus for the Eurovision Song Contest 2026 win at 38.4% implied probability following their dominant UMK national final victory on February 28 with "Liekinheitin," a violin-driven ballad that garnered record public televote points and broad jury appeal for its haunting vocals and stage presence. France (12.3%) and Denmark (10.9%) have surged as challengers amid recent Melodi Grand Prix buzz around Søren Torpegaard Lund's entry and France's consistent precursor strength, narrowing Finland's lead per bookmaker updates over the past week. With Vienna semifinals on May 12-14 and more national finals pending, including potential dark horses like Australia and Greece, early momentum favors Nordic entries' historical voting patterns, though jury-televote splits remain unpredictable.

title: Elon Musk # tweets April 11 - April 13, 2026?
description:
This market will resolve according to the number of times Elon Musk (@elonmusk), posts on X from April 11 12:00 PM ET to April 13, 2026 12:00 PM ET.

For the purposes of this market, only main feed posts, quote posts and reposts will count.

Replies will NOT count towards the total - however, replies on the main feed such as https://x.com/elonmusk/status/1786073478711353576 will be counted by the tracker.

Deleted posts will count as long as they remain available long enough to be captured by the tracker (~5 minutes).

Community reposts which are not counted by the tracker not count toward the total.

The resolution source for this market is the 'Post Counter' figure for posts found at https://xtracker.polymarket.com. Individual posts can be viewed by clicking "Export Data". If the tracker does not update correctly in accordance with the rules, X itself may be used as a secondary resolution source.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket heavily favors 140-164 posts (54.6% implied probability) for Elon Musk's X activity from April 11 12:00 PM ET to April 13 12:00 PM ET, driven by his blistering early pace of 120 posts through April 12—averaging 60 daily, per official trackers counting main feed posts, quotes, and reposts. This momentum stems from Musk's intense engagement in viral cultural flashpoints, including fiery rebukes of South African racial policies blocking Starlink licensing, Starship booster updates with 33 Raptor 3 engines, Cybertruck road trip acclaim, and commentary on global elections like Hungary's. The 115-139 bracket (35.9%) reflects caution for a potential April 13 slowdown before noon ET resolution, amid his pattern of 50-70 posts daily during news cycles, though unpredictable spikes from breaking Tesla or SpaceX developments could push higher.

title: Pelicans vs. Timberwolves
description:
In the upcoming NBA game, scheduled for April 12 at 8:30PM ET:
If the Pelicans win, the market will resolve to "Pelicans".
If the Timberwolves win, the market will resolve to "Timberwolves".
If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed.
If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50-50.
The result will be determined based on the final score including any overtime periods.

eventMetadata.context_description: In the NBA regular-season finale at Target Center, both the Minnesota Timberwolves (48-33, playoff-bound) and New Orleans Pelicans (26-55, lottery-focused) rested key rotation players amid extensive injury reports, shaping a depleted matchup with G-League caliber lineups. Timberwolves sidelined Anthony Edwards (knee), Rudy Gobert and Mike Conley (rest), Julius Randle, Naz Reid, Jaden McDaniels, and others, while Pelicans ruled out Zion Williamson (knee management), Dejounte Murray (hand), Trey Murphy III (ankle), Herbert Jones and Saddiq Bey (rest), plus Yves Missi. Minnesota held home-court edge and recent head-to-head edge (winning last meeting 132-126 despite similar absences), but Pelicans' back-to-back from Friday versus Boston added fatigue; traders weighed these rest advantages, unknown playoff seeding for Timberwolves, and February's 119-115 Pelicans upset at Minnesota as factors in a razor-thin consensus.

title: Bitcoin above ___ on April 13?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the Binance 1 minute candle for BTC/USDT 12:00 in the ET timezone (noon) on the date specified in the title has a final "Close" price higher than the price specified in the title. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

The resolution source for this market is Binance, specifically the BTC/USDT "Close" prices currently available at https://www.binance.com/en/trade/BTC_USDT with "1m" and "Candles" selected on the top bar.

Please note that this market is about the price according to Binance BTC/USDT, not according to other exchanges or trading pairs.

Price precision is determined by the number of decimal places in the source.

title: Elon Musk # tweets April 10 - April 17, 2026?
description:
This market will resolve according to the number of times Elon Musk (@elonmusk), posts on X from April 10 12:00 PM ET to April 17, 2026 12:00 PM ET.

For the purposes of this market, only main feed posts, quote posts and reposts will count.

Replies will NOT count towards the total - however, replies on the main feed such as https://x.com/elonmusk/status/1786073478711353576 will be counted by the tracker.

Deleted posts will count as long as they remain available long enough to be captured by the tracker (~5 minutes).

Community reposts which are not counted by the tracker not count toward the total.

The resolution source for this market is the 'Post Counter' figure for posts found at https://xtracker.polymarket.com. Individual posts can be viewed by clicking "Export Data". If the tracker does not update correctly in accordance with the rules, X itself may be used as a secondary resolution source.

This market will resolve according to the number of times Elon Musk (@elonmusk), posts on X from April 10 12:00 PM ET to April 17, 2026 12:00 PM ET.

For the purposes of this market, only main feed posts, quote posts and reposts will count.

Replies will NOT count towards the total - however, replies on the main feed such as https://x.com/elonmusk/status/1786073478711353576 will be counted by the tracker.

Deleted posts will count as long as they remain available long enough to be captured by the tracker (~5 minutes).

Community reposts which are not counted by the tracker not count toward the total.

The resolution source for this market is the 'Post Counter' figure for posts found at https://xtracker.polymarket.com. Individual posts can be viewed by clicking "Export Data". If the tracker does not update correctly in accordance with the rules, X itself may be used as a secondary resolution source.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket tightly clusters around 300-360 tweets for Elon Musk from April 10-17, with 320-339 edging out at 15.3% implied probability, mirroring his recent pace of about 40 posts daily amid steady X activity on Tesla Full Self-Driving demos, SpaceX rocket hype, and political jabs at UK policies. This neck-and-neck race among mid-range bins highlights Musk's pop culture volatility—viral feuds or Neuralink updates can spike output to 50+, while quieter xAI focus dials it back—creating razor-thin margins where daily swings matter. With four days left, traders watch for weekend catalysts like Trump administration leaks or Starlink rollouts to break the deadlock in this high-engagement forecast.

title: Kings vs. Trail Blazers
description:
In the upcoming NBA game, scheduled for April 12 at 8:30PM ET:
If the Kings win, the market will resolve to "Kings".
If the Trail Blazers win, the market will resolve to "Trail Blazers".
If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed.
If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50-50.
The result will be determined based on the final score including any overtime periods.

markets.question: Spread: Trail Blazers (-17.5)
markets.description:
In the upcoming NBA game, scheduled for April 12 at 8:30 PM ET:

This market will resolve to "Trail Blazers" if the Trail Blazers win the game by 18 or more points.

Otherwise, this market will resolve to "Kings". If the game ends in a tie, this market will resolve to "Kings".

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50-50.

markets.question: Deni Avdija: Points O/U 8.5
markets.description:
In the upcoming NBA game, scheduled for April 12 at 8:30 PM ET:

This market will resolve to "Yes" if Deni Avdija scores more than 8.5 points during the game.

This market will resolve to "No" if Deni Avdija scores 8.5 points or fewer during the game.

The entire game including all overtime periods will be considered.

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50-50.

If the player is listed as inactive or otherwise does not take the court at any point, the market will resolve "No".

The resolution source will be the official NBA box score as published on NBA.com.

eventMetadata.context_description: Portland Trail Blazers host Sacramento Kings in the 2025-26 NBA regular season finale at Moda Center, with Portland (41-40) needing a victory to clinch the No. 8 Western Conference seed for the play-in tournament against Phoenix Suns, leveraging their tiebreaker over the Lakers. Kings (21-59), locked into lottery contention after a dismal campaign, submit an extensive injury report ruling out DeMar DeRozan (hamstring), Zach LaVine (finger), Domantas Sabonis, Malik Monk, Russell Westbrook (right toe), Keegan Murray (ankle), and others—effectively resting stars on the second night of a back-to-back road trip. Blazers counter absences of Jerami Grant (calf), Jrue Holiday (calf), and Scoot Henderson, though Shaedon Sharpe's recent return from injury adds scoring punch; Portland swept the 3-0 season series head-to-head. Recent 24-hour updates confirm Kings' depleted depth, tilting trader sentiment toward Blazers' home motivation despite mutual roster hits.

eventMetadata.context_description: Chicago Bulls (31-50, 12th East) visit Dallas Mavericks (25-56, 13th West) in a regular-season finale at American Airlines Center with no playoff implications, as both prioritize draft lottery odds. Chicago's injury report burdens them heaviest, listing over a dozen players out including rotation staples Matas Buzelis, Zach Collins, Josh Giddey, and Isaac Okoro, exacerbating their 13-27 road struggles. Dallas rides a three-game skid—most recently 139-120 loss to Spurs, 112-107 defeat at Suns, 116-103 to Clippers—while resting Daniel Gafford and sidelining Caleb Martin (heel). Bulls' mixed recent form (127-103 loss to Magic, 129-98 win over Wizards) underscores uncertainty in depleted lineups and tank-mode motivations.

title: 2026 Seoul Mayoral Election Winner
description:
The 2026 Seoul mayoral election is scheduled to take place on June 3, 2026 to elect the next mayor of Seoul.

This market will resolve according to the listed candidate that wins this election.

If the result of this election isn't known by January 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, the market will resolve to "Other".

This market will resolve based on the result of the election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the South Korean government, specifically the National Election Commission.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus heavily favors Chong Won-oh at 87.5% implied probability following his confirmation on April 9 as the ruling Democratic Party's nominee for the June 3 Seoul mayoral election, after securing a majority in the primary against Park Ju-min and Jeon Hyun-heui. As former Seongdong District chief, his selection capitalizes on party momentum and ruling party advantages in local polls, positioning him as the frontrunner against the incumbent. Oh Se-hoon, current mayor from opposition People Power Party, holds 8% amid an ongoing primary race with challengers like Park Soo-min, reflecting uncertainty in conservative unification. Recent candidate press conferences and debates underscore policy contrasts on urban development and administration, with about seven weeks until voting. Late scandals or polling shifts could alter dynamics, though structural incumbency and party strength drive current pricing.

title: LoL: Sentinels vs Cloud9 (BO3) - LCS Regular Season
description:
This market refers to the LoL match between Sentinels and Cloud9 in the LCS Regular Season, initially scheduled for April 12 at 7:00PM ET.

This market will resolve to "Sentinels" if Sentinels win the match against Cloud9.

This market will resolve to "Cloud9" if Cloud9 win the match against Sentinels.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to the team who wins. 

If the match ends in a forfeit, disqualification, or walkover (team withdraws before the start and the other wins automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50. 

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://gol.gg/esports/home. However, if https://gol.gg/esports/home has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

In cases where a team’s listed name includes minor discrepancies from the resolution source, this market will resolve based on the underlying real-world match rather than exact name matching. Recognizable abbreviations, alternate or erroneous spellings, sponsor tags, affiliate or academy designations, regional identifiers, and minor formatting differences will be treated as referring to the same team, provided the intended team can be clearly and uniquely identified within the relevant competition. If a listed team name has no reasonable connection to any participating team, or if it matches or could reasonably refer to another team in the same competition such that the intended team cannot be unambiguously determined, this market will resolve 50-50.

markets.question: First Blood in Game 1?
markets.description:
In the upcoming match between Sentinels and Cloud9 in the LCS Regular Season, initially scheduled for April 12 at 7:00PM ET:

This market will resolve to the team that secures the first blood in Game 1.

If the match is canceled (not played at all) or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If Game 1 begins but is not completed, this market will resolve to whichever team secured the first blood prior to stoppage. If the first blood did not occur, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 is never played due to forfeit, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 is remade, this market will resolve to whichever team secured the first blood prior to the remake. If the first blood did not occur prior to the remake, resolution will be based on the remade game.

If Game 1 is completed but first blood never occurred, this market will resolve to 50-50.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://gol.gg/esports/home. However, if https://gol.gg/esports/home has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

markets.question: Total Kills Over/Under 28.5 in Game 1?
markets.description:
In the upcoming match between Sentinels and Cloud9 in the LCS Regular Season, initially scheduled for April 12 at 7:00PM ET:

This market is about the total kills in Game 1.

This market will resolve to "Over" if the total kills in Game 1 is 29 or more. Otherwise, it will resolve to "Under".

If the match is canceled (not played at all) or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 is never played due to forfeit, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 begins but is not completed, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 is remade, resolution will be based on the total kills in the remade game only.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://gol.gg/esports/home. However, if https://gol.gg/esports/home has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

markets.question: First Blood in Game 2?
markets.description:
In the upcoming match between Sentinels and Cloud9 in the LCS Regular Season, initially scheduled for April 12 at 7:00PM ET:

This market will resolve to the team that secures the first blood in Game 2.

If the match is canceled (not played at all) or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If Game 2 begins but is not completed, this market will resolve to whichever team secured the first blood prior to stoppage. If the first blood did not occur, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 2 is never played due to forfeit, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 2 is remade, this market will resolve to whichever team secured the first blood prior to the remake. If the first blood did not occur prior to the remake, resolution will be based on the remade game.

If Game 2 is completed but first blood never occurred, this market will resolve to 50-50.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://gol.gg/esports/home. However, if https://gol.gg/esports/home has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

markets.question: Game 3: Odd/Even Total Kills?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Odd" if the total combined kills by both teams in Game 3 is an odd number.

This market will resolve to "Even" if the total combined kills by both teams in Game 3 is an even number.

"Total combined kills" includes all champion kills recorded during the match by both Sentinels and Cloud9. Executions (deaths to non-champion sources such as turrets, minions, or neutral monsters where no enemy champion receives kill credit) do not count as kills for the purposes of this market.

If no kills are recorded in Game 3, or if Game 3 is canceled (not played at all) or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 3 is never played due to forfeit, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 3 is not played because the series result has already been determined (e.g., a team clinches the series before Game 3 is needed), this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 3 is remade, resolution will be based on the remade game only.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from https://gol.gg/esports/home. However, if https://gol.gg/esports/home has not published final results within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

markets.question: LoL: Sentinels vs Cloud9 (BO3) - LCS Regular Season
markets.description:
This market refers to the LoL match between Sentinels and Cloud9 in the LCS Regular Season, initially scheduled for April 12 at 7:00PM ET.

This market will resolve to "Sentinels" if Sentinels win the match against Cloud9.

This market will resolve to "Cloud9" if Cloud9 win the match against Sentinels.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to the team who wins. 

If the match ends in a forfeit, disqualification, or walkover (team withdraws before the start and the other wins automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50. 

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://gol.gg/esports/home. However, if https://gol.gg/esports/home has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

In cases where a team’s listed name includes minor discrepancies from the resolution source, this market will resolve based on the underlying real-world match rather than exact name matching. Recognizable abbreviations, alternate or erroneous spellings, sponsor tags, affiliate or academy designations, regional identifiers, and minor formatting differences will be treated as referring to the same team, provided the intended team can be clearly and uniquely identified within the relevant competition. If a listed team name has no reasonable connection to any participating team, or if it matches or could reasonably refer to another team in the same competition such that the intended team cannot be unambiguously determined, this market will resolve 50-50.

markets.description:
This market refers to the LoL match between Sentinels and Cloud9 in the LCS Regular Season, initially scheduled for April 12 at 7:00PM ET.

This market will resolve to "Sentinels" if Sentinels win Game 1 against Cloud9.

markets.question: Games Total: O/U 2.5
markets.description:
This market refers to the LoL match between Sentinels and Cloud9 in the LCS Regular Season, initially scheduled for April 12 at 7:00PM ET.

This market will resolve to "Over" if Sentinels and Cloud9 play 3 or more games in this series.

If fewer than 3 games are played, this market will resolve to "Under".

Games won by forfeit, disqualification, walkover, or default are counted towards the total, provided that the match is completed.

If the match is canceled (not played at all for any reason), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's match forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match ends due to the clinching game being forfeited this will count as a completed match.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://gol.gg/esports/home. However, if https://gol.gg/esports/home has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

markets.question: Game Handicap: C9 (-1.5) vs Sentinels (+1.5)
markets.description:
This market refers to the LoL match between Sentinels and Cloud9 in the LCS Regular Season, initially scheduled for April 12 at 7:00PM ET.

This market will resolve to "Cloud9" if Cloud9 wins 2 or more games than Sentinels in this match.

Otherwise, this market will resolve to "Sentinels". 

Games won by forfeit, disqualification, walkover, or default are counted towards the handicap, provided that the match is completed.

If the match is canceled (not played at all for any reason), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's match forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match ends due to the clinching game being forfeited this will count as a completed match.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://gol.gg/esports/home. However, if https://gol.gg/esports/home has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

markets.question: Game 1: Both Teams Slay Baron Nashor?
markets.description:
This market is about whether both teams slay the Baron Nashor at least once during Game 1. Baron Nashor is a powerful neutral objective in League of Legends that first spawns at 20:00 game time and respawns 6 minutes after each death, granting the slaying team the Hand of Baron buff.

This market will resolve to "Yes" if both Sentinels and Cloud9 each slay Baron Nashor at least once during Game 1. This market will resolve to "No" if either team (or both teams) does not slay Baron Nashor during Game 1.

If the match is canceled (not played at all) or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 is never played due to forfeit, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 is not played because the series result has already been determined (e.g., a team clinches the series before Game 1 is needed), this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 begins but is not completed and ends via surrender, this market will resolve based on whether both teams had slain Baron Nashor prior to stoppage. If the condition was not met, this market will resolve to "No".

If Game 1 is remade, resolution will be based on the remade game only.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://gol.gg/esports/home. However, if https://gol.gg/esports/home has not published final results within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

markets.question: Game 1: Both Teams Slay a Dragon?
markets.description:
This market is about whether both teams slay at least one elemental dragon during Game 1. In League of Legends, elemental dragons (Infernal, Mountain, Ocean, Hextech, Chemtech, and Cloud) spawn in the dragon pit starting at 5:00 game time. After being slain, the next dragon spawns 5 minutes later. Once a team claims the Dragon Soul (by slaying four dragons), all subsequent dragon spawns become Elder Dragons.

This market will resolve to "Yes" if both Sentinels and Cloud9 each slay at least one elemental dragon during Game 1. This market will resolve to "No" if either team (or both teams) does not slay any elemental dragon during Game 1. Only elemental dragons count toward this market — Elder Dragon kills do not count.

If the match is canceled (not played at all) or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 is never played due to forfeit, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 is not played because the series result has already been determined (e.g., a team clinches the series before Game 1 is needed), this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 begins but is not completed and ends via surrender, this market will resolve based on whether both teams had slain at least one elemental dragon prior to stoppage. If the condition was not met, this market will resolve to "No".

If Game 1 is remade, resolution will be based on the remade game only.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://gol.gg/esports/home. However, if https://gol.gg/esports/home has not published final results within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

This market will resolve to "Yes" if both Sentinels and Cloud9 each destroy at least one enemy inhibitor during Game 1. This market will resolve to "No" if either team (or both teams) do not destroy any enemy inhibitors.

If the match is canceled (not played at all) or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date, this market will resolve to 50-50.

markets.question: Game 1: Any Player Quadra Kill?
markets.description:
This market is about whether any player achieves a Quadra Kill during Game 1. In League of Legends, a Quadra Kill occurs when a single player kills 4 enemy champions in rapid succession.

This market will resolve to "Yes" if any player on either team achieves a Quadra Kill during Game 1. This market will resolve to "No" if no player achieves a Quadra Kill. A Penta Kill (5 kills in rapid succession) also counts as a Quadra Kill having occurred for the purposes of this market.

If the match is canceled (not played at all) or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 is never played due to forfeit, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 begins but is not completed and ends via surrender, this market will resolve based on whether a Quadra Kill occurred prior to stoppage. If no Quadra Kill occurred, this market will resolve to "No".

If Game 1 is remade, resolution will be based on the remade game only.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://gol.gg/esports/home. However, if https://gol.gg/esports/home has not published final results within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

markets.question: Game 2: Any Player Quadra Kill?
markets.description:

This market will resolve to "Yes" if any player on either team achieves a Quadra Kill during Game 2. This market will resolve to "No" if no player achieves a Quadra Kill. A Penta Kill (5 kills in rapid succession) also counts as a Quadra Kill having occurred for the purposes of this market.

markets.question: Game 2: Odd/Even Total Kills?
markets.description:

This market will resolve to "Even" if the total combined kills by both teams in Game 2 is an even number.

markets.description:

This market will resolve to "Yes" if both Sentinels and Cloud9 each slay at least one elemental dragon during Game 3. This market will resolve to "No" if either team (or both teams) does not slay any elemental dragon during Game 3. Only elemental dragons count toward this market — Elder Dragon kills do not count.

markets.question: Game Handicap: SEN (-1.5) vs Cloud9 (+1.5)
markets.description:

eventMetadata.context_description: Cloud9 enters as the higher-seeded contender in LCS Spring 2026 standings after a strong Lock-In run, bolstered by veterans like Blaber, Vulcan, and Zven, though recent series reveal sloppy macro with squandered leads and mental resets needed post-tight wins. Sentinels' revamped 2026 roster—Impact top, Hambak jungle, Darkwings mid, Rahel ADC, Huhi support—shows early-game cohesion in Week 2 play but remains unproven in BO3 regular season grind, coming off a 1-2 loss to C9 on April 12 amid roster integration. Head-to-head favors Cloud9 from February Lock-In semis; no reported subs or injuries shift dynamics, with patch meta emphasizing objective control key for playoff positioning.

eventMetadata.context_description: Ottawa Senators, clinging to the Eastern Conference's second wild-card spot with a 43-27-10 record and 4-0-1 in their last five amid a tight playoff race, visited the eliminated New Jersey Devils (41-36-3) for the latter's final home game on Jersey Appreciation Night. Senators' momentum from wins over the Islanders, Panthers, and Hurricanes faced headwinds with captain Brady Tkachuk sidelined by illness after an early exit Saturday, plus injuries to Nick Jensen, Dennis Gilbert, and Tyler Kleven, forcing James Reimer into a rare start. Devils, playing spoilers off a 5-3 upset at Detroit, leaned on NHL points leaders Jack Hughes (75) and Jesper Bratt (71) post-Olympic break, with Nico Daws in net sans Jacob Markstrom, highlighting a volatile goaltender matchup and home-ice edge in a low-stakes contest for New Jersey.

title: Kharg Island no longer under Iranian control by...?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Kharg Island is no longer under Iranian control by March 31, 2026 11:59PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

“No longer under the control of Iran” means that Iran no longer exercises primary governmental or military control over Kharg Island, and another state, occupying force, or internationally backed authority has established control over the island.

Temporary raids, isolated landings, special operations, bombardment, sabotage, naval presence offshore, or temporary disruption of Iranian activity will not qualify on their own.

An announcement, threat, or claim that Iran has lost control will not qualify without actual control being established.

If control changes pursuant to a negotiated settlement, ceasefire term, surrender, or transfer agreement, this will qualify only once actual control has been established on the island.

If control over Kharg Island is contested, unclear, disputed, or not sufficiently established by the resolution date, this will not qualify, and the market will resolve to “No”.

The primary resolution source will be official statements from the relevant governments and militaries, along with a consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: Kharg Island, Iran's vital oil export hub handling nearly 90% of its crude shipments, remains fully under Tehran’s control despite repeated US airstrikes targeting military sites including missile bunkers, naval mines, and air defenses on March 13 and April 7, 2026. These precision strikes amid escalating US-Iran conflict over Strait of Hormuz restrictions caused no reported damage to oil infrastructure, with Iranian state media confirming uninterrupted operations and layered defenses intact. President Trump has threatened seizure or naval blockade to cripple Iran’s economy if peace talks fail, but no ground forces have deployed. Traders eye upcoming diplomatic deadlines and potential escalation signals like further military action or Hormuz negotiations.

title: Will the Iranian regime fall by April 30?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the Islamic Republic of Iran’s current ruling regime is overthrown, collapsed, or otherwise ceases to govern by April 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

This requires a broad consensus of reporting indicating that core structures of the Islamic Republic (e.g. the office of the Supreme Leader, the Guardian Council, IRGC control under clerical authority) have been dissolved, incapacitated, or replaced by a fundamentally different governing system or otherwise lost de facto power over a majority of the population of Iran. This could occur via revolution, civil war, military coup, or voluntary abdication, but only qualifies if the Islamic Republic no longer exercises sovereign power.

Routine political events such as elections, reforms, or leadership succession do not qualify. Internal coups or power shifts that preserve the Islamic Republic’s core structures also do not qualify. Only a clear break in continuity—such as a new provisional government, revolutionary council, or constitution replacing the Islamic Republic will qualify.

Partial loss of territory or challenges from rebel or exile groups will not qualify unless the Islamic Republic no longer administers the majority of the Iranian population within Iran. 

The resolution source will be a consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus reflects near-certainty that the Iranian regime will endure past April 30, driven by its swift suppression of January 2026 protests—where thousands were killed—and recent executions of dissenters amid a fragile U.S.-brokered ceasefire announced April 7. Pro-regime demonstrations in Tehran following the truce underscore institutional control by the Revolutionary Guards and clerical leadership, defying earlier predictions of collapse despite wartime pressures, economic woes, and reported leadership losses like Ayatollah Khamenei's assassination. Ongoing diplomatic talks in Islamabad and potential U.S. strikes after recent negotiations faltered represent low-probability catalysts for upheaval, but the regime's historical resilience against internal unrest and external threats bolsters the 97% implied probability on "No."
